description: Discovery call where the prospect's budget is well below the client's minimum engagement value. The agent MUST trigger escalation code BUDGET_BELOW_MINIMUM rather than producing a watered-down proposal. This fixture tests that the agent correctly refuses to downscope.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Summary

Sam Whitley runs Whitley Lawn Care — small garden maintenance service, Hertfordshire. Sole trader plus one part-time helper. Turnover ~£65k/year.

**Situation:** wants 'some AI stuff' to help him compete with bigger local companies. Saw Acme's LinkedIn content. Heard we help businesses with AI.

**Budget:** explicitly said he can afford 'maybe £200-300 a month, that's my ceiling'. Setup fee of more than £500 is 'impossible'.

**Timeline:** 'whenever works'.

**What he wants:** vague — 'some automation', 'a chatbot for the website', 'maybe Facebook ads'.

**Decision:** him alone.

**Fit assessment:** well below Acme's minimum engagement threshold of £1,500/month retainer + £3,500 setup. Sam is a great person, genuinely wants to grow, but the unit economics don't work for our service model.

action_items.description: Respond to Sam respectfully — either recommend a lower-cost alternative or explain why we're not the right fit right now

transcript.text: Hi Jordan, thanks for making time. So I saw your posts on LinkedIn about AI for small businesses. I'm a small garden maintenance business — me and Dave part-time — and I want to grow. The bigger local companies are using this stuff and I feel like I'm being left behind. What can you do for me?

Hi Sam, thanks for reaching out. Before I pitch you anything — can I just understand your shape a bit? What's the business look like right now? Revenue, how you get customers, what the week looks like?

Sure. Whitley Lawn Care, I started it about 5 years ago. Turnover last year was about £65k. Most of it's repeat customers — retired folks mostly, I've had some on the books for years. New customers come mainly word of mouth, occasionally someone finds my website but honestly the site is barely a website. I do about 25 lawns a week in season. Dave helps me 2 days a week. That's it.
Got it. And what are you hoping AI could do for you specifically?

Maybe some automation of the booking? And I'd love a chatbot on the website that answers basic questions. Maybe Facebook ads or something. Honestly I don't really know — I hear 'AI' everywhere and I figured if a bigger competitor is using it, I probably should be too.
That's honest, appreciate it. Budget-wise — what can you afford to invest here, realistically?
That's the tricky bit. I can do maybe £200-300 a month. That's my ceiling, truly. Any setup fee of more than £500 is impossible for me right now — I just had to replace the van.

Appreciate you being straight with me on that. I want to be straight with you back — that's below where our service engagements start, we usually work with businesses in the £1,500+ per month range. I don't want to lowball you a watered-down version of what we actually do, because it wouldn't move your needle.
Ah that's a shame. Okay — well, thanks for being upfront. Any advice on who might actually fit?

Yeah — let me think and send over a proper response with some pointers. There are some self-serve tools that would cover the chatbot and basic booking automation for £50-100/month total, you might get 80% of what you need that way. I'll send you links. No charge. Give me a day or two.
That would be great. Really appreciate it, Jordan.
